follow_ups.title: JobRequest(None) contract violations crash coordinator
follow_ups.body:
Seven instances of JobRequest(None, ...) violate the constructor contract: job parameter must be AgentJob|BuildTestJob|GitJob, not None. Coordinator will crash attempting to execute these placeholders.

Affected locations:
- ci.py:82 (_poll PENDING path returns JobRequest(None, 'POLL'))
- ci.py:96 (_request_fix returns JobRequest(None, 'PUSH_READY'))
- ci.py:100 (_request_push returns JobRequest(None, 'POLL'))
- merge_wait.py:109 (_poll PENDING path returns JobRequest(None, 'POLL'))
- merge_wait.py:114 (_request_dirty_resolve returns JobRequest(None, 'POLL'))
- merge_wait.py:119 (_request_blocked_address returns JobRequest(None, 'PUSH_BLOCKED_READY'))
- merge_wait.py:123 (_request_learn returns JobRequest(None, '_FINISH_PASS'))

Fix: Replace with real job submissions (AgentJob for agent work, GitJob for git operations) or use Continue/StageOutcome outcomes to avoid job submission where appropriate.

follow_ups.title: Module-level I/O import violates pipeline architecture (AC1)
follow_ups.body:
ci.py:13 imports gh_pr_checks at module level, violating AC1 requirement (zero I/O imports in stage modules) and pipeline architecture principle that all I/O must be coordinator-owned and injected via StageContext.

The gh_pr_checks call at ci.py:76 should be mediated through ctx.github.pr_checks() accessor instead. This requires:
1. Add pr_checks(pr_number) method to StageGitHub protocol in base.py
2. Move import to method-local scope in _poll()
3. Update coordinator (#1817) to implement the new accessor

rejected.title: Implement on_job_done routing logic
rejected.reason: Current placeholders (pass statements) are intentional — real job routing depends on coordinator (#1817) integration and job result types not yet finalized in this scope.

rejected.title: Add stage-level integration tests
rejected.reason: Stage tests (test_stage_ci.py, test_stage_merge_wait.py) depend on JobRequest contract fixes first; testing invalid JobRequest(None) would validate broken behavior.
